what_it_does: Implements a stateless discovery adapter that runs a CLI's --help output and parses it into CapabilityEntry records, supporting both option-row and subcommand-list parse rules.
observations: Uses regex to parse Commander.js/Cobra/clap-style option rows generically, without hardcoding flag names.
Supports two parse_rule branches: 'option-row' for flags and 'subcommand-list' for Commands sections.
Flag ids include the '--' prefix, e.g. 'flag/--debug'.
The discover method runs a subprocess command with optional stderr merging and a configurable timeout.
Extracts aliases, argument placeholders, choices, default values, and a heuristic value_type.
Skips the universal 'help' pseudo-command when parsing subcommands.
References a floor_guard contract in the docstring but does not enforce it inside this file.
standouts: parse_rule 'subcommand-list' extends coverage from flags to subcommands as capabilities
Flag ids deliberately retain the '--' prefix
floor_guard is described as a contract but not implemented locally
conventions: Heavy docstrings explaining contracts and format assumptions
Regex constants defined at module level
Fail-loud behavior on empty output or unknown parse_rule
Generic shape-based parsing rather than name-based parsing
Type hints and dataclass-driven output via CapabilityEntry
concerns: floor_guard is mentioned but not enforced in this file, so under-parsing could go undetected if the caller omits it
Regex heuristics may break on wrapped option descriptions or non-standard indentation
_arg_type classification is heuristic and may mislabel argument types
Subcommand section detection may match lines containing 'commands' outside actual command lists
notes: A generic CLI help parser that turns --help text into structured capability entries for flags and subcommands, with a strong fail-loud philosophy but local regex fragility.
questions: Where is the floor_guard value defined and which component enforces it?
What does the CapabilityEntry schema require beyond the fields shown?
How are platform_id and version supplied by the caller?
Is stderr_merge intended to default to True or False for most sources?
How are multi-line wrapped descriptions handled after the first option row?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it parses option rows and subcommands from --help text. The only mismatch is that floor_guard is documented but not enforced inside this file.
intention_signals: The file tries to convert unstructured CLI --help output into a versioned, structured capability registry without embedding knowledge of any specific tool's flags.
novelty: Explicit parse_rule branching for both flags and subcommands, plus a deliberate id convention that preserves the '--' prefix on long flags.
contribution: A reusable adapter that extracts both flags and subcommands from CLI help text into typed CapabilityEntry records, together with a subprocess runner that honors stderr merging and timeouts.
summary_for_embedding: A generic CLI help discovery adapter that parses --help option tables and command sections into CapabilityEntry records using regex heuristics.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a platform-agnostic, fail-loud mechanism to turn live CLI help output into structured capability registry entries.

symbols.does: Stateless discovery adapter selected by DiscoverySource.type 'cli-help'.
symbols.description: The class defines source_type, runs a subprocess to capture --help text, and dispatches parsing to option-row or subcommand-list rules, producing CapabilityEntry records.

symbols.does: Executes the configured command, substituting {binary}, and returns raw --help text.
symbols.description: Runs the command with optional stderr merging and timeout, failing loudly if the command produces no output, leaving parsing to the parse method.

symbols.does: Dispatches raw help text to the chosen parse rule and returns a list of CapabilityEntry objects.
symbols.description: Supports 'option-row' for flag tables and 'subcommand-list' for command sections; raises on unknown rules and builds entries with ids, aliases, descriptions, defaults, and value types.

symbols.does: Parses an option spec column into canonical name, alias, argument placeholder, and choices.
symbols.description: A static helper that splits comma-separated flag tokens, identifies long vs short flags, extracts <arg> or [arg] placeholders, and splits pipe-separated choices.

symbols.does: Heuristically maps an argument placeholder to a value_type string.
symbols.description: A static helper that returns empty for no argument, 'enum' for choices, 'csv' for variadic placeholders, 'path'/'json'/'int'/'string' based on substring matching.
